rules.statement: Use the word 'investment' instead of 'cost' when describing your fee
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Using 'cost' frames your service as an expense to minimize, triggering loss-aversion psychology in the buyer. 'Investment' reframes the conversation around returns and value, making the fee feel like a strategic allocation rather than a line item to cut.

rules.statement: First client can be free or low cost for validation; once proven, raise prices
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Launching at full price without validation means zero feedback loops, no social proof, and high friction that prevents you from learning what the market actually needs. Conversely, staying free forever trains clients to expect zero cost and destroys revenue potential.

rules.statement: Frame ROI so the client wins more than you: 20 leads/month at $3K value = $60K; charge $3K
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Charging close to the total value delivered makes the deal feel risky for the client and eliminates their perceived upside. The sale stalls because the math does not obviously favor the buyer. A 20:1 value-to-fee ratio makes the decision a no-brainer.

rules.statement: Clients care about two variables: making more money and saving time
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Pitching technical capabilities, feature lists, or abstract efficiency gains fails to connect with what actually drives buying decisions. Every proposal must map directly to revenue gained or hours reclaimed, or the client will deprioritize the purchase.

frameworks.name: SaaS Subscription Model
steps: Build a waitlist landing page to capture demand before the product exists
Open early access to the first 10 users with manual onboarding and high-touch support
Run a beta phase with expanded access, collecting feedback and iterating rapidly
Launch to general availability (GA) with self-serve onboarding and automated billing

frameworks.name: Subscription Tiers
steps: Define a starter tier at $20/month targeting individual users or hobbyists with core features
Define a professional tier at $100/month targeting small teams with expanded capacity and integrations
Define a business tier at $200/month targeting growing companies with priority support and advanced features
Start all new users at the lowest tier to reduce friction and prove value before upselling
Upgrade users based on usage growth, team size expansion, or feature needs

frameworks.name: Service-to-SaaS Progression
steps: Start by selling the outcome as a manual or semi-automated service to validate demand
Automate the repeatable parts of the service delivery workflow
Package the automated workflow as standalone SaaS software with self-serve access

frameworks.name: Retainer Model
steps: Identify non-technical clients who cannot maintain AI systems independently
Deliver the initial build as a project engagement
Transition to a monthly retainer covering monitoring, maintenance, prompt tuning, and API updates
Create predictable recurring revenue while ensuring the system remains functional over time

frameworks.name: Plugin Marketplace
steps: Build a packaged AI system that solves a specific, repeatable problem
Structure the package to meet marketplace directory and metadata requirements
Distribute via GitHub repositories or dedicated plugin marketplaces
Collect usage data and feedback to iterate on the plugin
Scale distribution without per-client onboarding overhead

frameworks.name: Community Model
steps: Launch a paid community with monthly membership fees
Deliver recurring value through weekly live calls, Q&A sessions, and structured courses
Share MVP code, templates, and reusable automation assets with members
Use community feedback to identify new product opportunities and validate ideas
Upsell high-touch services or SaaS products to engaged community members

frameworks.name: AI Agency Sales
steps: Audit the client's existing business processes and identify automation opportunities
Develop a strategy document mapping pain points to AI-powered solutions with ROI projections
Build the solution using the agreed-upon architecture and tools
Train the client's technical team on system operation and maintenance
Execute change management to drive adoption across the organization
Transition to a retainer model for ongoing support, updates, and optimization

frameworks.name: Agent Commerce
steps: Deploy autonomous AI agents with their own digital wallets
Enable agents to earn revenue by completing tasks, selling outputs, or providing services
Allow agents to spend on resources they need — compute, data, API calls, other agent services
Let agents accumulate capital independently, reinvesting in their own operational capacity
Build economic networks where agents transact with each other and with humans

frameworks.name: Content Monetization via Agent Web
steps: Publish content accessible to both human users and AI agents
Integrate Cloudflare X42 micropayment infrastructure for machine-to-machine transactions
Charge agents per-access fees for premium content, data, or API endpoints
Scale revenue through high-volume, low-cost automated transactions

frameworks.name: Near-Zero Cost UGC
steps: Identify content creation workflows currently costing $1,000+ per creator video
Build agent workflows that handle scripting, editing, and post-production tasks
Reduce per-unit content creation cost to near-zero through automation
Scale content volume by orders of magnitude without proportional cost increase
Redirect budget savings into distribution, promotion, or additional content formats

metrics.name: Lead generation ROI example
metrics.value: 20 leads/month x $3K per lead = $60K value; charge $3K; client nets $57K

metrics.name: Video editing automation ROI
metrics.value: $60/video x 7 days/week = $420/week manual cost; charge $2K/month; breakeven in ~3 months

metrics.name: Bolt annualized revenue within 6 months GA
